The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog. This is a simple sentence for testing purposes.

Speed reading is a technique that helps people read faster while maintaining comprehension. The key is to reduce subvocalization and increase the visual span of each fixation.

RSVP stands for Rapid Serial Visual Presentation. It displays one word at a time at a fixed point, eliminating the need for eye movement. Studies show that trained readers can achieve speeds of 500 to 1000 words per minute using this technique.

However, comprehension may decrease at very high speeds, especially with complex or technical material. Finding the right balance between speed and understanding is essential for effective speed reading.
